title: Review a lock-free or atomic change
description: Verify the happens-before reasoning, ABA safety, and detector evidence before accepting clever lock-free code.
body: Steps: 1. Identify every shared atomic and the ordering it claims (seq-cst, acquire/release, relaxed). 2. For each, trace the happens-before edges that make a reader observe the writer's prior stores. 3. Check compare-and-swap loops for the ABA problem (value cycles back) and add tagging/hazard pointers if needed. 4. Demand a stress test and a race-detector clean run as evidence. 5. Compare against a simpler locked version's benchmark to confirm the complexity is justified. Rollback point: keep the locked implementation available to revert to. Validation: detector is clean, the stress test passes, and the ordering argument is written down.
